name: Lorentz invariance in the electron sector (SME)
requirement: Any candidate framework must respect Lorentz invariance in the electron sector to the precision set by laboratory comagnetometer, ion-trap, and atomic-spectroscopy experiments. SME electron-sector coefficients (b_e)^mu, (c_e)^mu nu, (d_e)^mu nu, and (g_e)^lambda mu nu are bounded at levels typically 10^-29 - 10^-33 GeV depending on the specific component.

regime.description: Tabletop laboratory experiments (atomic clocks, ion traps, comagnetometers) plus astrophysical electron-photon vacuum bounds; sensitivity to direction-dependent and energy-dependent electron propagation.

current_bound.value: |(b_e)^X|, |(b_e)^Y| < 10^-31 GeV (comagnetometers); |(c_e)^TT|, |(c_e)^XY| < 10^-19 (Ca+ ion trap); various other components bounded at 10^-29 - 10^-33 GeV
current_bound.experiment: Co-magnetometer (Smiciklas et al.), Ca+ ion trap (Pruttivarasin et al.), Yb+ ion clock comparison
current_bound.reference: Pruttivarasin et al., 'Michelson-Morley analogue for electrons using trapped ions to test Lorentz symmetry', Nature 517, 592 (2015), arXiv:1412.2194; Smiciklas et al., 'New test of local Lorentz invariance using a 21Ne-Rb-K comagnetometer', PRL 107, 171604 (2011); compiled bounds in Kostelecky-Russell SME data tables

what_it_excludes: Together with photon-sector and proton/neutron-sector SME bounds (covered in lorentz_invariance_photon, vacuum_birefringence_polarization, cpt_kaon_mass_difference), constrains all components of fermion Lorentz violation at typically 10^-29 GeV or better. Closes most natural Planck-suppressed Lorentz-violating scenarios except for those with additional suppression mechanisms.

parameterization.name: Standard Model Extension (SME) electron-sector Lagrangian extension, including CPT-odd coefficients (a_e, b_e) and CPT-even coefficients (c_e, d_e, e_e, f_e, g_e, H_e).
parameterization.reference: Colladay and Kostelecky, PRD 58, 116002 (1998), arXiv:hep-ph/9809521; Kostelecky and Lane, PRD 60, 116010 (1999); Kostelecky and Russell, RMP 83, 11 (2011), arXiv:0801.0287 (data tables).
parameterization.summary: Each SME coefficient enters the Dirac Hamiltonian and produces specific signatures: spectral shifts, sidereal modulation, magnetic-field dependence.

procedural.predicted_observable: Energy splittings, transition frequencies, comagnetometer phase shifts as functions of laboratory orientation and time of day (sidereal modulation).
procedural.bound_in_parameterization: Component-by-component bounds tabulated in Kostelecky-Russell SME data tables (annual update at arXiv:0801.0287).
framework_inputs: If the framework breaks Lorentz invariance in the electron sector, decompose the modification onto SME basis coefficients and confront with the relevant entry in the data tables.
Include possible Planck-suppressed Lorentz-violating operators (dimension >= 5 in the SME).

primary_references.citation: Pruttivarasin et al., 'Michelson-Morley analogue for electrons using trapped ions to test Lorentz symmetry', Nature 517, 592 (2015)
Smiciklas et al., 'New test of local Lorentz invariance using a 21Ne-Rb-K comagnetometer', PRL 107, 171604 (2011)
Kostelecky and Russell, 'Data Tables for Lorentz and CPT Violation', RMP 83, 11 (2011) (annual updates)
